item.title: Former Treasury boss says Australians left with little to show from commodities boom
item.description: Former Treasury secretary Ken Henry says the under-taxed resources sector has hollowed out Australia's economy, and raising more revenue from the sale of LNG resources was in the national interest.

item.title: Preparing food for future use with a pressure cooker
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Main offender' dingo euthanised after multiple attacks in Karijini National Park
item.description: A male dingo has been "humanely destroyed" after multiple attacks within days of each other at Western Australia's famous Karijini National Park.

item.title: Mother of woman who died at Bandyup Women's Prison questions if daughter had adequate care
item.description: Deaths in custody are on the rise and family members and advocates for a woman who died in a Perth prison say more needs to be done to keep inmates safe.

item.title: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild on the Coorong, but scientists are hunting for more
item.description: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild, in crushed limestone on the side of a road. But scientists are hunting for more.

item.title: Why women are getting less sleep than men — and what we can do about it
item.description: There are biological and psychological reasons behind why women experience more disrupted sleep than men, but experts say the mental load is another powerful yet less obvious contributor.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 27: Have you got the memory to beat our quiz?
item.description: Which Australian city played host to Expo 88? And what age did the world's oldest spider reach?

item.title: Trump is political kryptonite in Australia but diplomacy still guides the way
item.description: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

item.title: China's 'quirky' fix for property crisis rewards marathon runners, AI experts and tennis players
item.description: Local governments in China are offering marathon runners, AI experts and tennis players subsidies to purchase unwanted properties amid a lingering real estate downturn.

item.title: The 'groundbreaking' AI tool helping Victorian rangers protect native species in a fraction of the time
item.description: A new AI tool will help Parks Victoria manage native and endangered species affected by natural disasters, including the summer bushfires, with the department making the technology available to everyone.

item.title: Why street artist Peter Drew decided to bring back his 'Aussie' poster series
item.description: Peter Drew has been hanging his controversial posters around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

item.title: Subdivisions taking place in Adelaide's 'visionary' Hills Face Zone
item.description: The Hills Face Zone was legislated to protect the natural character of Adelaide's landscape backdrop but, despite subdivisions not being permitted, there have been 11 over the past decade.

item.title: Platypuses need threatened species recognition in NSW, researcher says
item.description: A leading researcher is calling for the platypus to be recognised as a threatened species in NSW, saying a lack of data is the only reason it has not been reclassified.

item.title: AI decision review system being trialled in Darwin women's division one cricket competition
item.description: Darwin's premier women's cricket league becomes the first Australian grade competition to use an artificial intelligence umpire review system.

item.title: Esteban Andrada punches Jorge Pulido, sparking Aragonese derby brawl between Real Zaragoza and Huesca
item.description: The Aragonese derby between Real Zaragoza and Huesca descends into a wild brawl after Zaragoza goalkeeper Esteban Andrada punches Huesca skipper Jorge Pulido in the face.

item.title: Bad Company: Art and big business collide in new Anne Edmonds, Kitty Flanagan comedy
item.description: Everything Anne Edmonds has done over the last two decades has been leading to Bad Company, an unfettered satire of the theatre world the comedian created, wrote and stars in alongside Kitty Flanagan.

item.title: Japan's north struck by magnitude-6.1 earthquake, followed by wildfires prompting evacuations
item.description: A preliminary magnitude-6.1 earthquake struck northern Japan early today, the US Geological Survey has reported, but there are no immediate reports of fatalities or damage.

item.title: Kerry Kennedy thought of father's assassination when shooter rushed security at gala dinner
item.description: Human rights activist Kerry Kennedy praises a "superhero" member of Congress for shielding her when an active shooter attempted to storm the annual White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

item.title: WA's far north 'blindsided' by Boskalis's marine sand proposal and lack of consultation
item.description: A dredging company acknowledges concerns from some community members in northern Western Australia that public meetings should have been held earlier.

item.title: PNG prime minister encouraged by Jarome Luai's visit and meeting with Chiefs bosses
item.description: The Wests Tigers playmaker is the Chiefs' top target ahead of their NRL entry in 2028, and the Papua New Guinea prime minister says his trip to PNG shows the interest is mutual.

item.title: Indigenous veteran Di Ryder slams 'distracting' booing at Anzac Day Welcome to Country
item.description: The Whadjuk Noongar elder who was booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth's Anzac Day dawn service says she had a right to speak at the event and will not back down.

item.title: Snowfall in April, record hot day in May. Is this a new norm for Tasmania? Scientists explain
item.description: Tasmania is well into autumn, but firefighters remain on alert as late-season bushfires have threatened communities. This Friday marks the first day of May, and with it could come one of the state's hottest May days in nearly 140 years of record-keeping.

item.title: Prediction markets accused of insider trading and profiting from state secrets
item.description: A US soldier accused of using classified information to place bets on a covert military operation has thrown a spotlight on the fast-growing world of prediction markets.

item.title: RSL Australia to review Welcome to Country policies after elders booed on Anzac Day
item.description: RSL Australia will review its Welcome to Country guidelines after Indigenous elders were booed during Anzac Day services across the country.

item.title: Australia's minimum fuel reserves would double under $800m Coalition plan, at a cost to drivers
item.description: Australia would have 60 days' worth of fuel supplies in reserve under an $800 million Coalition proposal that would cost drivers one cent per litre.

item.title: Train collision near Jakarta kills 14 as rescuers complete evacuation
item.description: The train operator says the crash, on the outskirts of Jakarta, involved a commuter train and a long-distance train.

item.title: The security breach that exposed Donald Trump to a shooting incident
item.description: A shooting incident at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has prompted a swirl of questions about how an alleged shooter breached Secret Service security and got so close to the US president.

item.title: Penny Wong will have to navigate China, Japan and South Korea tensions
item.description: When Foreign Minister Penny Wong travels to Japan, China and South Korea this week, she will move through a region where relationships point in different directions at the same time.

item.title: Australian rugby may benefit from copying Crusaders' lateral thinking with Leicester Fainga'anuku
item.description: Thinking outside the square has previously served Australian rugby well, which is why there are lessons it can take away from at least one of New Zealand's teams in Super Rugby Pacific.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood delivers IPL masterclass as Royal Challengers Bengaluru smash Delhi Capitals
item.description: Australian pace maestro Josh Hazlewood delivers his best performance of the IPL season, taking four wickets to spearhead another RCB win.

item.title: New York mayor announces free FIFA World Cup fan zones as costs continue to escalate
item.description: New York City will host free events for soccer fans who can't afford the hefty ticket prices for the FIFA World Cup, Mayor Zohran Mamdani says.

item.title: Push to make voluntary assisted dying available through telehealth
item.description: Despite voluntary assisted dying being legal in Victoria, many regional patients are not getting the same end-of-life care.

item.title: CCTV to be installed on Gold Coast waterways to crack down on illegal boaties
item.description: The Gold Coast Waterways Authority says the initiative comes after more than 400 unregistered and unseaworthy vessels were removed from the city's waterways over the past five years.

item.title: Donald Trump calls for broadcaster to fire Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: Donald Trump says Jimmy Kimmel should be fired for a monologue he delivered days before the third alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: Broken PBS system behind failure to list Mounjaro for type 2 diabetics, according to Medicines Australia
item.description: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

item.title: Suspect in Washington dinner shooting charged with trying to assassinate Donald Trump
item.description: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

item.title: What I've learnt about living on other mobs' Country — respectfully
item.description: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

item.title: Coalition points to next-generation stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stopgap
item.description: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

item.title: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop trio The Ronettes, dies aged 80
item.description: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop band the Ronettes, who sang the enduring hits Be My Baby, Baby I Love You, and Walking in the Rain alongside her cousins, has died aged 80.

item.title: Donald Trump names former Republican congressman as new US ambassador to Australia
item.description: The Trump administration has nominated former congressman David Brat as ambassador to Australia.

item.title: Alex Johnston and Jarome Luai set to join PNG Chiefs in major get for NRL expansion team
item.description: Record-setting South Sydney try-scoring winger Alex Johnston looks set to become the first player to sign for the PNG Chiefs ahead of their 2028 NRL entry.

item.title: Farmers and authorities on alert as Australian plague locust migrates to South Australia
item.description: Multiple locust swarms in South Australia's Riverland region have put farmers and authorities on alert, prompting the South Australian Plague Locust Commissioner to send surveying teams to the region.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Big tech hits back at Labor government's Media Bargaining Incentive plan
item.description: US tech giant Meta has condemned Labor's plan to tax large digital platforms that fail to pay for using Australian journalism as "government-mandated transfer of wealth".

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Netball's powerful all-female statement on Anzac Day
item.description: They both struggle for recognition in male-dominated fields, so Super Netball chose to host an all-female Anzac Day ceremony to commemorate and give visibility to female veterans.

item.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
item.description: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

item.title: Australia lacks national strategy to regulate AI spread in workplace, report states
item.description: Australia risks repeating the mistakes it made with social media unless it moves quickly to regulate the spread of artificial intelligence in the workplace, according to a new report.

item.title: Plastic packaging price pain could push up costs for shoppers on everything from milk to make-up
item.description: Companies that use plastic packaging are facing price pressures and supply disruptions, which they warn will likely mean higher costs for consumers. The cost of food-grade resin out of Singapore, which is used in milk bottles, has increased 110 per cent since the start of the Middle East war.

item.title: Amount of antibiotic florfenicol used at Tasmanian salmon farm lease concerning, say Greens, expert
item.description: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

item.title: Fuel security a 'tricky balancing act', experts say as budget nears
item.description: As the international fuel crisis continues to cause concern, the federal government is under pressure to provide a fix during next month's budget.

item.title: Queenslanders urged to get tested for viral hepatitis after liver cancer deaths triple since 1980s
item.description: An alarming new report has found liver cancer deaths have risen from 1.6 deaths per 100,000 people in the 1980s to 5.1 per 100,000 in 2018–2022.

item.title: Parents slam 'weak' response at The Friends' School after girls targeted in AI deepfake pornography scandal
item.description: Two parents of girls at a private school have criticised the institution's response to a deepfake incident, saying they were encouraged not to tell their daughters they had been identified in material shared in a boys' group chat.

item.title: El Niño warming could be unprecedented, but don't assume record drought in Australia
item.description: Australia's weather pattern is in a reset, and the weather events happening this month will act like the ingredients to determine the flavour of our climate for the rest of the year.

item.title: Early Farrer voters make choices known as pre-polling gets underway in blockbuster by-election
item.description: Pre-polling commences in the seat of Farrer for the hotly contested by-election triggered by the resignation of former opposition leader Sussan Ley.

item.title: Self-employment can help autistic people avoid workplace barriers
item.description: After the National Autism Strategy identifies the potential of self-employment to provide meaningful work for people with autism, neurodivergent people who are already self-employed say it can help overcome workplace barriers.

item.title: Marcus Stoinis hits half-century in Punjab Kings' six-wicket IPL loss to Rajasthan Royals
item.description: Ricky Ponting's Punjab Kings record a six-wicket loss against Rajasthan Royals in the IPL, despite Marcus Stoinis's breathtaking innings.

item.title: Why WA's unique history has it leading the pack on ADHD medication prescriptions
item.description: With WA home to seven out of Australia's top-10 highest ADHD prescribing neighbourhoods, experts, psychiatrists and doctors weigh in with their theories.

item.title: Hundreds of jobs to go at Home Affairs department in voluntary redundancy drive
item.description: The secretary of the second-largest department says she has been trying to reduce headcount by natural attrition, but that further job cuts are needed by the end of the financial year to fit within the department's budget.

item.title: Video of arson attack on Central Coast Pilates studio released by police
item.description: The video released by police captures the moment a man douses the Pilates studio in petrol before setting it alight.

item.title: VicGrid surveyors cancel access visit after farmgate protest makes them feel unsafe
item.description: Renewable planning body VicGrid has threatened a high-profile anti-transmission-line campaigner with enforcement action after government surveyors felt "hindered" from accessing his farm.

item.title: Jarome Luai confirms Wests Tigers exit to join PNG Chiefs for 2028 NRL season
item.description: Jarome Luai will leave Wests Tigers at the end of the 2027 season, announcing he will join the PNG Chiefs for their entry to the NRL.

item.title: Jarome Luai's move to Papua New Guinea makes the power of the Chiefs very real
item.description: Jarome Luai's decision to sign with Papua New Guinea is a showcase of the economic power behind the Chiefs and marks the start of the greatest rugby league gold rush since Super League.

item.title: Ultra-swimmer Andy Donaldson sets world record with 55km Ord River challenge
item.description: Andy Donaldson took less than 12 hours to swim the challenging 55-kilometre course down the Upper Ord River in Western Australia's north.

item.title: Mother who faked son's eye cancer diagnosis to raise money sentenced to over four years' jail
item.description: A mother who faked her six-year-old son's cancer diagnosis to raise thousands of dollars has been jailed for more than four years for the "cruel, calculated" and "manipulative" crime.

item.title: Health Minister says Canberra Hospital knife incident will be subject to a joint review
item.description: The ACT's chief police officer says the knife produced by a Canberra Hospital patient that led to an hours-long security incident had not been detected by police during a search of the man earlier in the day.

item.title: Twenty-five-million-year-old platypus fossils with teeth found in outback SA
item.description: Researchers have discovered ancient platypus fossils in outback South Australia, where they are believed to have lived with lungfish, flamingos and freshwater dolphins. They also had major a difference from the modern species: well-formed teeth.

item.title: Keith Lees faces court over alleged murder of Meaghan Louise Rose on Sunshine Coast in 1997
item.description: Police allege Keith Lees killed Meaghan Louise Rose, whose body was found at the base of cliffs on the Sunshine Coast in July 1997.

item.title: Northern Beaches Hospital returns to public ownership after death of Joe Massa
item.description: The NSW premier says a family's campaigning after their toddler died following treatment at Northern Beaches Hospital helped change public policy.

item.title: New South Wales still committed to saving Tomago Aluminium as negotiations stall
item.description: The NSW government remains committed to Tomago Aluminium but is still waiting to learn how much the bailout will cost.

item.title: Australia's richest person donates 'sexy' $1 million plane to Pauline Hanson's One Nation
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

item.title: AI deepfakes are circulating in our schools. What's the right way to handle them?
item.description: Deepfake content, made from artificial intelligence, is increasingly circulating in schools. But what can parents do if their children are the target?

item.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates
item.description: While inflation remains well above the Reserve Bank's target, there are details in the numbers that may give it reasons to hold off another rate hike in May.

item.title: Perth women share experiences of infection and surgery after nail salon visits
item.description: More Perth women share their experience of needing surgery after having an adverse reaction to nail salon services, triggering a warning from the consumer watchdog.

item.title: The six weeks from hell that whipped New South Wales into State of Origin shape
item.description: For the second year in a row both New South Wales and Queensland enter the women's State of Origin match with barely a game between them this year, but a six-week training block that one player called "one of the hardest things I've ever done" has them primed for the series opener.

item.title: FIFA allows Afghan women's football team to return to international competition
item.description: Afghan women footballers will be able to showcase their skills to the world for the first time in five years after FIFA cleared the way for their return to international competition.

item.title: High fuel prices hurting regional families taking kids to sporting fixtures
item.description: Country sporting clubs are rallying to help families attend fixtures as fuel prices double, with some travelling up to 640 kilometres. But it is an extra cost many will take on to maintain the sports are so integral to their communities.

item.title: SA Legislative Council result delay creating 'confusion and uncertainty'
item.description: With less than a week until state parliament resumes, an incoming upper house member says she still cannot access the building or appoint staff because the election result is yet to be declared.

item.title: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll opens up about mental health struggles in emotional post
item.description: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll reveals his mental health struggles in an emotional social media post, amid a push from players for the AFL to implement a mental health round.

item.title: APRA music awards 2026 winners announced, with Amyl and the Sniffers taking top gong
item.description: Punk rockers Amyl and The Sniffers have bagged three top gongs at the Australian music industry's APRA awards.

item.title: China inching towards resuming fuel exports as Penny Wong presses Australia's case
item.description: Chinese state-owned oil companies have agreed to negotiate directly with Australian companies on jet fuel after the foreign minister's visit to China.

item.title: Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraisers and travel to catch Rinehart jet to Florida
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

item.title: King Charles III visits September 11 memorial as he tours New York
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met survivors and family members of victims, as well as with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

item.title: See how the price of everything has changed with inflation
item.description: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

item.title: Coalition's risky Farrer strategy could clear path for One Nation
item.description: Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.

item.title: Lebanon ceasefire not working because free rein given to Israel, says deputy prime minister Tarek Mitri
item.description: Lebanese Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

item.title: Gambling among Australian construction workers well above national average, creating safety risks
item.description: New research reveals 89 per cent of construction workers have gambled in the past year — higher than the national average.

item.title: Matthew Pavlich and Steven Baker reflect on the infamous 'Sirengate' match in Launceston 20 years on
item.description: Two decades ago, two teams met at York Park in Launceston. The siren sounded but the match continued and the dreaded word "gate" was added to another footy scandal.

item.title: Will the OPEC oil cartel survive without the UAE?
item.description: The United Arab Emirates decision to pull the plug on its OPEC membership could be the nail in the coffin for an organisation that has struggled to keep its members in line for the past 66 years.

item.title: Technology should make life easier but it doesn't always for people with disability
item.description: While the newest home technology aims to make life easier, when it prioritises aesthetics over usability, some lives are actually made much harder, writes Nas Campanella.

item.title: Anastasia Potapova reaches Madrid Open semifinals to make WTA Tour history
item.description: Anastasia Potapova experiences an "explosion of emotions", as her remarkable run in Madrid continues at the prestigious tournament.

item.title: NT diphtheria outbreak worsens as cases of the potentially deadly disease more than double
item.description: Cases of a deadly, vaccine-preventable strain of diphtheria have now risen to 18, after the Centre for Disease Control first declared an outbreak last month.

item.title: Six WA Police officers sanctioned over treatment of Aboriginal man who died in Kalgoorlie prison
item.description: Six WA Police officers have been sanctioned over their treatment of a man in their custody who later died in prison, after the ABC revealed a constable dismissed his threat of suicide, and laughed at news of his death.

item.title: Travis Head helps Sunrisers score six-wicket IPL win over Indians
item.description: Travis Head reminds cricket fans of what he can produce with the bat, as he helps Sunrisers Hyderabad to a six-wicket win over Mumbai Indians.

item.title: Donald Trump's would-be assassin took hotel selfie while 'armed to the teeth', court told
item.description: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

item.title: Rare discovery of ambergris on Denmark beach excites scientists and historians
item.description: A highly prized, rare ingredient used in the production of luxury perfumes washes up on a Denmark beach — and it comes from the intestine of sperm whales.

item.title: Wastewater data suggests meth use is at a record high. Here's why we don't need to panic
item.description: At first glance, these findings sound alarming, but before we draw big conclusions about worsening drug use, it's worth taking a closer look at what this data can and can't tell us.

item.title: Does the 'Jessica' tantrum trend really work?
item.description: Whether it's at home or in the middle of a shopping centre, tantrums can test the patience of any parent or caregiver.

item.title: Mark Latham ordered to pay Alex Greenwich $100k and delete posts in vilification case ruling
item.description: The former One Nation MP has been found to have vilified openly gay politician Alex Greenwich in the second case centring around a dispute between the pair.

item.title: Two men denied bail after allegedly flying meth in drone in Wollongong
item.description: Police allege a drone was used to deliver meth in Wollongong before it crashed, leading to a wider investigation.

item.title: Melbourne teacher charged after camera found in primary school bathroom
item.description: The 33-year-old was arrested at the school in the city's south-east after the discovery of a camera in a bathroom.

item.title: Archibald Packing Room Prize 2026 won by Sean Layh for portrait of Jacob Collins
item.description: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

item.title: Miranda Priestly makes a splashy return in The Devil Wears Prada 2
item.description: The Devil Wears Prada 2 drops the beloved (or loathed) characters from 20 years ago into a very different media environment.

item.title: What is inflation and should you worry about it?
item.description: The latest inflation data is making headlines. Find out what it means for you in three minutes.

item.title: Counterterrorism intelligence's share of funding in 'significant' decline since 2020, royal commission finds
item.description: Royal commissioner Virginia Bell says classified documents reveal a significant shift in intelligence spending away from counterterrorism since 2020, in the first findings of what failings led to last year's Bondi terror attack.

item.title: Tense exchanges in court as judge turns screws on ACCC's case against Woolworths
item.description: There have been tense exchanges in the Federal Court, with the judge overseeing the bombshell case between the ACCC and Woolworths questioning the consumer watchdog's case.

item.title: Narrandera residents fed up with murky tap water
item.description: When residents of Narrandera, in southern NSW, turn on the tap, the water that comes out is sometimes so dirty it stains their clothes.

item.title: Melbourne City to face 'team we never want to lose to' in A-League Women semifinal
item.description: Taylor Otto sees a lot of potential in the Australian domestic league. She thinks it just needs a bigger investment to get there.

item.title: NT Police find body believed to be missing 5yo Alice Springs girl
item.description: Northern Territory Police have found a body believed to be that of a missing five-year-old Alice Springs girl, five days after she disappeared from a town camp.

item.title: Paramedic stabbed in Reservoir in Melbourne's north
item.description: Police have charged a man with attempted murder after he allegedly stabbed an on-duty paramedic in Melbourne's north.

item.title: Queensland man James Lake-Kusviandy Blee on trial over Newcastle cocaine haul
item.description: Queensland man James Lake-Kusviandy Blee is on trial over a cocaine shipment into Newcastle.

item.title: The Friends' School denies discouraging parents from telling victims about deepfake incident
item.description: The principal of a Tasmanian private school denies claims by two parents that the school ever tried to discourage them from telling their daughters they had been identified in AI deepfake images.

item.title: Ex-Vandemonian Touring music promoter Brendan Self charged with cocaine, cannabis offences
item.description: The former managing director of Hobart events company Vandemonian Touring appears in court charged with trafficking cocaine and possessing cannabis.

item.title: Global Sumud Flotilla carrying Australians intercepted on its way to Gaza by IDF
item.description: The Global Sumud Flotilla has been intercepted by the IDF, an official tweet by the group says.

item.title: RACQ denies misleading customers with insurance notices amid ASIC Federal Court lawsuit
item.description: When the corporate regulator accused RACQ of misleading customers with insurance renewal notices, the insurer apologised. Now, it is defending the notices in court filings.

item.title: Chalmers does little to stop capital gains tax debate as budget looms
item.description: Seven years ago, proposed changes to capital gains tax and negative gearing were named as part of the reason Labor lost the unlosable election. Times have changed.

item.title: Victorian bottle shops awash with illicit alcohol, researchers find
item.description: The National Drug Research Institute found 30 per cent of bottle shops it audited across different areas of Melbourne and regional Victoria were stocking illicit alcohol.

item.title: Moore goals after siren as Hawks and Pies settle for thrilling draw
item.description: A goal after the siren from Dylan Moore allows Hawthorn to claim a draw against Collingwood at the MCG.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: NRL coach Craig Bellamy diagnosed with form of neurodegenerative disorder
item.description: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy has been diagnosed with a form of neurodegenerative disorder, the club has said.

item.title: Australia in talks over latest US proposal to open Strait of Hormuz, but PM says 'no determination' made
item.description: Australia has begun discussions with the United States about joining the latest US proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as the Trump administration tries to build an international coalition to restore freedom of navigation in the critical waterway.

item.title: How two 'Origin plays' turned the tide for New South Wales against Queensland
item.description: With Queensland on the verge of another classic ambush, it took two "Origin plays" for New South Wales to turn the tide in the women's series opener in Newcastle.

item.title: Alice Springs erupts in violence after 5yo girl's alleged killer arrested
item.description: Wild scenes erupted on the streets of Alice Springs last night following the arrest of Jefferson Lewis over the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Guy Montgomery went from lazy school student to hosting a TV spelling bee across two countries
item.description: Guy Montgomery is back with the third season of his inimitable Spelling Bee, with brand-new sidekick Sam Campbell. We turn the tables on the Kiwi comic to ask him the tough questions.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A Trump shooting scare, a stunt gone wrong, a burger blue and a rescued roo
item.description: It's time to sit back after a big week in news and test how much you remember.

item.title: Seasonal produce for all your autumn soups, roasts and salads
item.description: From carrots to cauliflower, here is some of the best seasonal produce to enjoy in your autumn soups, roasts and salads.

item.title: Bond yields at crisis level as Australian government's 10-Year Treasury rate passes 5 per cent
item.description: The 10-Year Treasury bond, which the Commonwealth issues to raise money, pushes past 5 per cent, raising the cost of paying off debt and suggesting inflation is not under control.

item.title: The home battery rebate is changing. Is it still worth it and what's the right size?
item.description: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

item.title: ACCC case against Woolworths winds up as spotlight remains on integrity of grocery pricing
item.description: As the Woolies federal court case wraps up and the Coles matter awaits judgment, here's what we've learned so far from the high-stakes cases.

item.title: The Bondi royal commission interim report is careful but ultimately damning
item.description: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

item.title: Commanders to brief Trump on fresh military options for Iran war — as it happened
item.description: Iran says it will respond with "long and painful strikes" on US positions if Washington renews attacks and reasserts its control over the Strait of Hormuz — as it happened.

item.title: New photo of Aung San Suu Kyi released as military says she is moved from prison to house arrest
item.description: Myanmar's detained former president Aung San Suu Kyi has been moved from prison to house arrest, according to state media.

item.title: National average house prices rose slightly in April, the slowest pace of growth since January 2025
item.description: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

item.title: China using a double-insurance strategy to secure crude oil supplies amid Iran war
item.description: China relies heavily on Middle Eastern oil imports, but experts say a double-insurance strategy appears to have helped the country absorb at least the initial shock after disruption to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Pacific nations aim to tackle regional unrest with expanded Pacific Response Group
item.description: The Pacific Response Group is beginning negotiations on a treaty that would allow it to respond more quickly to natural disasters and other crises across the region.

item.title: The algal bloom may be 'gone' but regional South Australians are grappling with a changing environment
item.description: While testing has shown a dramatic improvement in South Australia's algal bloom situation, industry and experts fear it won't be a quick recovery.

item.title: 'No secrets': Alternative school's history of discipline in spotlight
item.description: For 50 years, this alternative school sold parents a vision of progressive education. But some who have passed through its doors say a 'cult-like' culture enabled decades of unchecked physical abuse.

item.title: 'Children have ended up on main roads', SA childcare regulator says
item.description: South Australia’s childcare centre watchdog says children have injured themselves or "ended up on main roads" due to poor supervision and that one in five services in the state do not meet national quality standards.

item.title: Once-safe Coalition seat Farrer now in sights of One Nation at by-election
item.description: A fascinating four-cornered contest is brewing as voters in the southern NSW seat of Farrer head to the ballot box for the second time in 12 months.

item.title: Mount Isa City Council moves to sell homes over unpaid rates in outback mining city
item.description: In Mount Isa, nearly 50 properties, some abandoned, have accrued years of unpaid rates and could be forced to sale.

item.title: At a Melbourne hospital, nitrous oxide users are presenting with psychosis
item.description: Harms from nitrous oxide have been growing in Australia in recent years, with the substance linked to a growing number of accidental deaths, while a study from a Melbourne hospital shows abuse of the inhalant is associated with psychosis.

item.title: Taylor says government is in 'denial' about situation in NT town camps — as it happened
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says the federal government is in "denial" about the situation in town camps following the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Saudi Arabia confirms it will no longer fund LIV Golf as future of league hangs in the balance
item.description: Australia's Cameron Smith is one of several big names in golf left with an uncertain future after Saudi Arabia pulled funding of LIV Golf.

item.title: ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions led by miners, ANZ shares slump despite profit jump — as it happened
item.description: The ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions but ANZ's profit announcement drags down the banks.

item.title: Extreme heat the most deadly yet preventable natural disaster, NSW Net Zero Commission says
item.description: An agency set up by the NSW government just two years ago says extreme heat is the most deadly yet most preventable natural hazard facing Australians.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood smashed for 24 off one over as Shubman Gill fires in the IPL
item.description: Shubman Gill takes Australian quick Josh Hazlewood to task as Gujarat took down Bengaluru in the IPL.

item.title: Emma Thompson stars in a new film made for an orchestral performance of Holst’s The Planets
item.description: A new silent film starring Emma Thompson and Greg Wise will accompany the Sydney Symphony Orchestra and Holst’s The Planets in a world-premiere performance.

item.title: Left in the red dirt: The forgotten trail of Australia's longest strike
item.description: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

item.title: Sea creatures, Anzac Day and AI camera woes feature in top WA photo picks for April
item.description: From dawn services to record-breaking swims, here are our top photo picks for Western Australia in April.

item.title: Reza Adib, partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, charged with rape
item.description: Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, has been charged with three counts of rape relating to alleged offending against a woman in her 30s at Burleigh Heads on March 30.

item.title: US Congress approves bill to end record Homeland Security funding shutdown
item.description: "It is about damn time," said one Democrat as US politicians voted to end the funding freeze for most of the department's agencies after Mr Trump's deadly immigration operations in Minneapolis.

item.title: Remote patient monitoring technology saving time, travel and money for regional mums during pregnancy
item.description: Remote health technology is saving regional patients time and money, despite many Victorian hospitals still running on paper records.

item.title: Construction ends on $915 million Parramatta Powerhouse museum set to open this year
item.description: Four years after work started, the highly anticipated Western Sydney museum is ready for the installation of five exhibitions, including a major show about space exploration.

item.title: Adelaide showed how good LIV Golf could be, but it was built on a flawed premise
item.description: While Adelaide showed just how good LIV Golf could be, the flawed premise of the breakaway tournament always spelled trouble.

item.title: From Foo Fighters to Jessie Ware and Nine Inch Noize — these are April's best albums
item.description: Come for Dave Grohl and co's latest. Stay for club-facing collabs, lush disco, homegrown releases and a pair of Québécois aliens making left-field instrumental jams.

item.title: Future of Northern Territory ambulance service in doubt as paramedics reach breaking point
item.description: A woman was left to suffer a seizure for two hours due to ambulance services being overwhelmed by call-outs earlier this month. The organisation's new chief executive has said unless more resources are funded to ease demand, the service could collapse.

item.title: Victoria pledges to close legal 'loophole' to prevent attacks on paramedics
item.description: Ambulance Victoria and the paramedics union say some people who attack paramedics have escaped mandatory jail terms due to the law's definition of "on-duty".

item.title: A deadline for Trump to end the Iran war is looming. He may seek to get around it
item.description: A deadline for US President Donald Trump to end the Iran war is about to expire. Here is what to expect.

item.title: Jobs at stake at Nyrstar Port Pirie and Hobart as assistance package ends
item.description: South Australia's premier says there is a "national imperative" to keep smelters running, as a $135 million assistance package for Nyrstar operations at Port Pirie and Hobart expires.

item.title: Putin condemns Ukrainian drone strikes against oil refineries deep inside Russian territory
item.description: Ukraine says its drones have struck an oil refinery deep inside Russia, marking the latest in a series of attacks on oil facilities behind the front lines as Kyiv seeks to curb Moscow’s energy revenues used to fund the war.

item.title: Poliovirus linked to overseas transmission detected in Perth wastewater
item.description: Poliovirus has been detected in a sample taken from a wastewater in Perth, the Health Department reveals.

item.title: No 'legal barrier' blocking ISIS-linked families returning to Australia, law expert says
item.description: A week since a group of 13 Australian women and children with ties to former Islamic State fighters left a Syrian refugee camp for Australia, they remain in the capital Damascus in a state of limbo.

item.title: SA government needs certainty on LIV Golf by October, premier says
item.description: The SA Premier says the government is "monitoring" the situation regarding LIV Golf closely, and would need a guarantee of its future in Adelaide by October.

item.title: Tense moment at FIFA Congress as Gianni Infantino tries to get Israeli and Palestinian delegates to shake hands
item.description: FIFA boss Gianni Infantino is criticised as he attempts to encourage Israeli and Palestinian delegates to shake hands.

item.title: Ricky Stuart says it is time Craig Bellamy put himself first after the NRL coach's shock diagnosis
item.description: Raiders coach Ricky Stuart says Craig Bellamy needs to start putting himself first after the Storm revealed the coaching legend had been diagnosed with a neurological condition.

item.title: AFL score reviews under scrutiny with some coaches questioning the point
item.description: Nobody wants to see their team robbed by a missed officiating decision. But as scrutiny over video assistant refereeing or umpiring continues, some are wondering whether it's all worth it.

item.title: More IP, more content, more jobs: What we learned at Screen Forever 40
item.description: Now in its 40th year, the annual Screen Forever conference attracts hundreds of industry leaders to look at the past, present and future of Australian screen content.

item.title: Man found guilty of negligent driving causing death over crash with motorcyclist on Canberra road
item.description: Sean Joshua Walton was driving his truck and turning right at an intersection when he crashed into a black Harley Davidson motorbike being driven by 47-year-old Craig O'Neil.

item.title: Terrorism threat level rises, man arrested after stabbing of two Jewish men in the UK
item.description: Britain raises its national terrorism threat level to "severe" from "substantial", a day after an antisemitic stabbing attack in north London, as Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer vows stronger action to protect Jewish people.

item.title: SA Police confirm remains found at Inman Valley belong to Jeff Mundy
item.description: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

item.title: Dyson Daniels and Mitchell Robinson ejected in Hawks' 140-89 loss to Knicks in NBA play-offs
item.description: There is little support for the Hawks' Australian star Dyson Daniels after he is ejected from Game 6 of their NBA play-off game against the Knicks.

item.title: How NSW halfback Jesse Southwell, a future Brisbane hero, broke Queensland's hearts
item.description: In her final act as a Novocastrian before heading to Brisbane, Jesse Southwell kicked the first field goal of her life to deliver victory for New South Wales in Origin I.

item.title: Billions of NDIS funds lost to 'integrity leakage' last year, executive tells parliamentary hearing
item.description: About $3.7 billion of National Disability Insurance Scheme money was handed out inappropriately last financial year, the scheme's integrity chief has told a parliamentary hearing.

item.title: Mastermyne Crinum Operations fined $7m after 2021 on-site death of Graham Dawson
item.description: Graham Dawson, 62, was killed in a roof collapse on a central Queensland mining site in 2021. The District Court in Brisbane heard Mastermyne Crinum Operations was criminally negligent and the death was avoidable.

item.title: Trump not keen on wearing body armour as Secret Service says agent not shot by friendly fire
item.description: US President Donald Trump has declined to wear a bulletproof vest amid fresh concerns about his safety following a shooting at a gala dinner on Saturday.

item.title: Matt Glaezter, cancer survivor, world champion and two-time Olympic medallist, confirms retirement
item.description: Matt Glaetzer was, for so long, the nearly man of cycling, heartbreakingly missing out on an elusive Olympic medal despite reaching five finals. But the South Australian refused to be beaten, either by that, or by cancer.

item.title: Banksy confirmed behind new London statue of man blinded by flag
item.description: Elusive street artist Banksy has confirmed he is behind a new work drawing crowds in central London: a statue of a suited man striding into the void while holding a flag that blocks his view.

item.title: Wollongong tradesman jailed for robbing paedophile in Lightning Ridge heist
item.description: A Wollongong man drove to Lightning Ridge with his police officer friend to rob an elderly convicted paedophile of $100,000.

item.title: Outback WA shire demands trucks get off road after beloved teacher killed in crash
item.description: The death of a beloved school teacher in a crash with a road train has prompted a call to review the safety of an unsealed road in remote WA.

item.title: Family of allegedly murdered 5yo calls for calm as police reveal vigilante attack left suspect unconscious
item.description: The family of Alice Springs five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby have called for calm following rioting in the outback town overnight, while police say her alleged murderer was beaten unconscious.

item.title: Suns coach Damien Hardwick fires back at Saints' Ross Lyon's AFL draft comment
item.description: Gold Coast coach Damien Hardwick provides a spirited response to St Kilda counterpart Ross Lyon's comment about the AFL national draft.

item.title: Supreme Court finds Perth man David Moussa not guilty of father's murder on insanity grounds
item.description: A Perth man who fatally stabbed his father has been found not guilty of murder on the grounds of insanity.

item.title: Port Pirie locals worried about jobs as Nyrstar smelter's future in doubt
item.description: Locals say the possible closure of the Port Pirie smelter and the loss of the 900 jobs associated with it would have ongoing effects on the city and the wider Spencer Gulf region.

item.title: Kingswood Primary School teacher accused of installing bathroom camera denied bail
item.description: Parents of children attending Kingswood Primary School filled the Moorabbin Magistrates Court on Friday, intending to see accused teacher Timothy Blamires.

item.title: Review finds fire ant eradication can succeed if flaws in 'mistrust and misinformation' are fixed
item.description: An independent review finds fire ant eradication is still achievable, but major issues, including poor communication, public mistrust and strategy confusion, must be fixed first.

item.title: Beaches closed near Wollongong as whale carcass removed at Bellambi
item.description: The sperm whale carcass was towed from Era Beach in the Royal National Park to Bellambi Boat Ramp in Wollongong this afternoon.

item.title: Kalgoorlie couple who underwent IVF treatment in shock after welcoming quadruplets
item.description: A Kalgoorlie couple expecting triplets welcome a surprise fourth baby — delivering an extraordinarily rare set of quadruplets that stunned even their doctors.

item.title: Retiring ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker on mistakes, rehabilitation and compassion
item.description: Heart is not something anyone expects from the court system, but it was something outgoing ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker, who retired in March after 15 years in the job, aimed to portray.

item.title: Live: Storm at risk of historic low as Dolphins fight back in Brisbane
item.description: Melbourne Storm fullback Sualauvi Faalogo has scored one of the best tries this NRL season, running 85 metres for a tremendous solo score. Follow live.

item.title: Rebel Wilson defamation trial told Charlotte MacInnes was 'fudging' her version of producer incident
item.description: Rebel Wilson's defamation trial hears from another producer on her directorial debut, who claims The Deb's lead actor was "fudging" her version of events about an alleged incident during production.

item.title: Mother's grief after toddler dies in apparent murder-suicide in Lismore
item.description: A coronial inquest hears letters that arrived three days after an apparent murder-suicide in Lismore outlined a father's deadly plans.

item.title: Family stunned after welcoming quadruplets in rare IVF birth
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Family of Tiwi Islands sistergirl calls for life sentence for killer Andrew Mungatopi
item.description: The family of a beloved Tiwi Islands sistergirl has pleaded for the man who killed her to be handed a life sentence, describing more than a year of sleepless nights, heartbreak and grief.

item.title: School traffic warden hit by delivery truck at Australind left with serious injuries
item.description: The man in his 80s was working a school zone crossing in Australind, near Bunbury in WA, when he was hit by the Woolworths truck.

item.title: NT government criticised for snubbing youth justice and incarceration Senate inquiry
item.description: The inquiry heard calls for more scrutiny over how Commonwealth money is spent in the NT, which is trending backwards on seven Closing the Gap targets.

item.title: Former Hunter high school teacher jailed for sexually assaulting teenage boy
item.description: A woman who groomed and sexually abused a teenage boy while she was working as a high school teacher near Newcastle has been sentenced to at least two years in prison.

item.title: Planning and Environment Court approves Sheraton high-rise development for Hervey Bay
item.description: Activists appealed the local council's approval of the 18-storey resort on the grounds that it would endanger local wildlife. The community fears the court's green light for the development will permanently change their seaside town.

item.title: May temperature records fall across Victoria as unseasonably warm weather continues
item.description: Weather records fall across the state, as a spell of summer-like conditions tips into the first day of May.

item.title: Live: Freo responds to coach's blasting as umpires hear boos over Butters goal
item.description: The injury-hit Western Bulldogs welcome a high-flying Fremantle side to Docklands as Adelaide and Port Adelaide meet in the Showdown. Follow live.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Chinese-owned Landbridge launches international legal action over forced sale of Port of Darwin
item.description: Anthony Albanese pledged at the federal election that Labor would return the port to Australian hands, saying it necessary for national security.
